§6Coke§f

§aHigh-efficiency fuel that burns hotter and longer — essential for steelmaking and torch production!§f

Coke is a petroleum product produced by heating coal or petroleum fluids in the absence of oxygen. It burns more efficiently than raw coal and generates more heat. Three variants exist that are fully interchangeable in recipes: §bPetroleum Coke§f, §bCoal Coke§f, and §bLignite Coke§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Combination Oven§f (coal route):
  §b–§f Coal Briquette → §e1×§f Coal Coke + §e150mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
  §b–§f Lignite Briquette → §e1×§f Lignite Coke + §e100mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
  §b–§f Coal Powder → §e1×§f Coal Coke + §e100mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
  §b–§f Lignite Powder → §e1×§f Lignite Coke + §e50mB§f Coal Tar Creosote.
  §b–§f Oil Tar / Crack Oil Tar → §bPetroleum Coke§f.
§b• Coker Unit§f (petroleum route): Various petroleum fluids → §bPetroleum Coke§f + coker fluids.
§b• Burner Press§f: Briquette coal/lignite powder with a Flat Stamp, then smelt.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Steel Alloying§f: Increases steel yield per iron ore.
§b• Torch Crafting§f: Produces §e2×§f the torches of regular coal.
§b• Graphite§f: Press Petroleum Coke in a Burner Press with Flat Stamp → §e1×§f Graphite Ingot.
§b• Fuel§f: Burns hotter than coal — ideal for Heating Ovens and Burner Presses.

§eTip§f: §bPetroleum Coke§f is the most efficient to produce at scale via the Coker Unit. For early game, the §bCombination Oven§f route using coal briquettes gives valuable §bCoal Tar Creosote§f as a byproduct — a useful liquid fuel for your Fluid Burners!
